title: acer italum var. opulifolium
intro: Acer italum var. opulifolium, also known as Italian maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the family Sapindaceae. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Acer italum var. opulifolium include its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The herbal preparation of this plant is typically made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are used to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, colds, and fever, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
